title: cymbopogon nardus var. goeringii
intro: Cymbopogon nardus var. goeringii, also known as East Indian lemongrass, is a variant of the lemongrass plant, a tropical grass native to India and Sri Lanka. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cymbopogon nardus var. goeringii include its ability to aid digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, essential oils, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
